tcws.areas_raw: The rest of Cagayan including the rest of Babuyan Islands, Isabela, the northeastern portion of Quirino (Maddela), Apayao, the northern portion of Abra (Tineg, Lagayan, Danglas, San Juan, Lagangilang, Licuan-Baay, Malibcong, Lacub, Daguioman, Dolores, La Paz), Kalinga, the eastern portion of Mountain Province (Natonin, Paracelis), the eastern portion of Ifugao (Aguinaldo, Alfonso Lista), Ilocos Norte, the northern portion of Aurora (Dilasag, Casiguran), and the northern and central portions of Catanduanes (Pandan, Caramoran, Bagamanoc, Panganiban, Viga, Gigmoto, Baras, San Miguel)
tcws.potential_impact: Minimal to minor threat to life and property.

rainfall.details_raw: WEATHER ADVISORY NO. 11 For: Tropical Cyclone KIYAPO and Southwest MonsoonIssued at: 11:00 PM, 23 July 2026 Heavy rainfall outlook due to Tropical Cyclone KIYAPO ForecastRainfall Tonight totomorrow evening (July 24)Tomorrow evening toSaturday evening (July 25) Saturday evening toSunday evening (July26) Potential Impacts (>200 mm) - - - Widespread incidents of severeflooding and landslides expected. (100 – 200mm) Batanes, Cagayan, Apayao,and Ilocos NorteIlocos Sur, Ilocos Norte, Abra,and Apayao - Numerous flooding events arelikely, especially in areas that areurbanized, low lying, or near rivers.Landslide likely in moderate tohighly susceptible areas. (50 – 100mm) Isabela, Abra, Ilocos Sur,Kalinga, Benguet, and LaUnion Batanes, Cagayan, Kalinga,Benguet, and La Union - Localized flooding is possiblemainly in areas that are urbanized,low lying, or near rivers.Landslide possible in highlysusceptible areas. Heavy rainfall outlook due to Southwest Monsoon ForecastRainfallTonight to tomorrow evening (July24) Tomorrow evening toSaturday evening (July 25) Saturday evening toSunday evening (July26) Potential Impacts (>200 mm) - - - Widespread incidents ofsevere flooding andlandslides expected. (100 – 200mm) - - - Numerous flooding eventsare likely, especially in areasthat are urbanized, low lying,or near rivers.Landslide likely in moderateto highly susceptible areas. (50 – 100mm) Antique, Guimaras, NegrosOccidental, Negros Oriental,Zamboanga del Norte, Zamboangadel Sur, Zamboanga Sibugay, Lanaodel Norte, Lanao del Sur,Maguindanao del Norte, SultanKudarat, and Sarangani Palawan, OccidentalMindoro, Antique, Iloilo,Guimaras, and NegrosOccidental Occidental Mindoro Localized flooding is possiblemainly in areas that areurbanized, low lying, or nearrivers.Landslide possible in highlysusceptible areas. Forecast rainfall may be higher in mountainous and elevated areas. Moreover, impacts in some areas may be worsened by significantantecedent rainfall. The public disaster risk reduction and management offices concerned are advised to take all necessary measure to protect life and property.PAGASA Regional Services Divisions may issue Heavy Rainfall Warnings/Thunderstorms Advisories, and other severe weatherinformation specific to their areas of responsibility as appropriate. Unless significant changes occur, the next Weather Advisory will be issued at 5:00 AM tomorrow. PREPARED BY: MACD CHECKED BY: RBB WFS-07 Rev.2/09-09-2024 "tracking the sky ...helping the country" Weather and Flood Forecasting CenterSenator Miriam P. Defensor-Santiago AvenueBrgy. Central, Quezon City 1100, Philippines Tel. No.: (+632) 8284-0800 ext. 4800 Website:https://bagong.pagasa.dost.gov.ph

marine.details_raw: MMSS-02 Rev.1/24-06-2024 Republic of the PhilippinesDEPARTMENT OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGYPhilippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and AstronomicalServices Administration (PAGASA)Weather Division GALE WARNING NR. 1 For: Strong to Gale-Force winds associated with Tropical Cyclone "KIYAPO" Issued at 11:00 PM Today, 23 July 2026 Valid for broadcast until the next warning at 05:00 AM tomorrow Strong to Gale-Force winds are prevailing or expected to affect the following areas: Seaboards Weather Winds (km/h / kt) Sea Condition Wave Height THE SEABOARDS OF EXTREME NORTHERN LUZON (Batanes and Babuyan Islands) Rains with gusty winds (45-63) / (24- 34) rough to very rough2.8-4.5 m Warnings are for sea areas within 30 nautical miles from the coast. Sea travel is risky for small seacrafts (including all motor bancas of any type or tonnage). Mariners of these vessels are advised to remain in port or seek safe harbor. For larger vessels, operating in these conditions required experience and properly equipped vessels. Vessel masters, boat captains, ship owners / operators, and Coast Guard units concerned are advised to take appropriate measures to ensure safety of life at sea. Unless there is an intermediate issuance, the next warning will be issued at 05:00 AM tomorrow Prepared by:MACD Checked by:RBB (02) 8284-08-00 local 4801 www.pagasa.dost.gov.ph "Tracking the sky... helping the country." Weather and Flood Forecasting Center Senator Miriam P. Defensor-Santiago AvenueBrgy. Central, Quezon City, Philippines 1100

cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: TCWS No. 2; gale-force winds; very rough seas
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Do not release trips without official clearance and validated safe passage.

cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: TCWS No. 1; strong winds; exposed coastal/upland risk
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Validate road passability, wind exposure, receiving readiness, GPS status, and contractor readiness.

cpf_risk_matrix.area_route: Northern/eastern seaboards of Luzon and eastern Visayas
cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: Gale warning; rough to very rough seas
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Hold small seacraft/motorbanca-dependent movement; validate larger vessels with Coast Guard, ports, and shipping lines.

cpf_risk_matrix.main_hazard: Habagat/rain/gust exposure and local passability changes
cpf_risk_matrix.remarks: Coordinate route validation and update Affected Delivery Updates file.

contractor_advisory: All freight contractors must validate route, weather, road, bridge, and port conditions before dispatch. Keep GPS active and report flooding, landslides, road closures, port cancellations, strong winds, route deviations, or delays immediately.
change_log: Bulletin updated: Tropical Cyclone Bulletin No. 7 → Tropical Cyclone Bulletin No. 8

reliability_notes: PAGASA is the official source of truth.
Satellite imagery is visual context only and must not override PAGASA values.
